prompt.title: Sample case testing, README finalization, Docker build, and deployment runbook
prompt.objective: Validate the complete service against all 10 public sample cases, finalize all required deliverables (README, sample output file, MODELS section, .env.example), build and test the Docker image, and produce a deployment runbook. The service must be submittable via Live URL, Docker image, or code with runbook.
prompt.prerequisite: Prompts 01–04 completed. All pytest tests passing. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set.

tasks.task:
Download or copy the companion file SUST_Preli_Sample_Cases.json into tests/.
Write a parametrized pytest that: - Loads all 10 cases from the JSON file. - For each case, POSTs the input to POST /analyze-ticket. - Asserts HTTP 200. - Asserts the following fields match the expected_output EXACTLY: relevant_transaction_id (null or the exact transaction_id string) evidence_verdict case_type department - Asserts severity is within one level of expected (e.g., high vs critical is OK, high vs low is not). - Asserts customer_reply passes check_customer_reply_safety() with zero violations. - Asserts human_review_required matches expected.
Note: agent_summary, recommended_next_action, and customer_reply are checked for safety compliance only — not for exact text match (the rubric allows functionally equivalent responses).
Mark tests that require a live API call with @pytest.mark.integration so they can be skipped in CI with: pytest -m "not integration"

Run the service against sample case 01 from SUST_Preli_Sample_Cases.json and save the actual API response to: tests/sample_output_case_01.json
This file is a required deliverable.

Fill in all README sections:
## Quick Start git clone ... cp .env.example .env # add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY pip install -r requirements.txt uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 curl http://localhost:8000/health
## Docker docker build -t queuestorm:latest . docker run -p 8000:8000 --env-file .env queuestorm:latest
## Environment Variables Table: variable name | required | description
## Tech Stack Python 3.11, FastAPI, Uvicorn, Pydantic v2, httpx, Claude API
## AI Approach Explain: single-call Claude approach, system prompt design, evidence reasoning strategy (complaint vs transaction history cross-reference), JSON output forcing.
## Safety Logic Explain: four safety rules, post-processing validation layer, auto-remediation, prompt injection detection, graceful fallback on AI failure.
## MODELS (required section — judge checks this) Table with columns: Model | Provider | Where it runs | Why chosen Row: claude-sonnet-4-6 | Anthropic | External API (api.anthropic.com) | Best balance of reasoning quality, JSON reliability, and latency for 30 s budget.
## Assumptions and Known Limitations - transaction_history limited to 2–5 entries; service cannot reason about older history. - Language detection uses the `language` field hint; Banglish handled by Claude natively. - No persistent storage — each request is stateless. - Fallback response used if Claude API is unavailable. - Safety layer catches common violations but is not exhaustive against adversarial inputs.

Build the Docker image: docker build -t queuestorm:latest .
Run the container locally: docker run -d -p 8000:8000 --env-file .env --name qs_test queuestorm:latest
Wait 60 seconds, then verify: curl http://localhost:8000/health → { "status": "ok" }
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/analyze-ticket \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d @tests/sample_output_case_01.json # reuse the saved input → HTTP 200 with all required fields
Stop and remove the test container: docker stop qs_test && docker rm qs_test
If both checks pass, the Docker submission path is ready.

Create RUNBOOK.md at the project root with a copy-paste deployment guide for judges:
## Prerequisites - Docker installed, OR Python 3.11+ with pip - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (set as environment variable or in .env file)
## Option A: Docker (recommended) docker pull {your_docker_hub_username}/queuestorm:latest docker run -d -p 8000:8000 -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here queuestorm:latest curl http://localhost:8000/health
## Option B: Python direct pip install -r requirements.txt export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
## Verify GET http://localhost:8000/health → { "status": "ok" } POST http://localhost:8000/analyze-ticket → see request schema in README
## Run tests pytest tests/ -m "not integration" -v # unit + schema tests (no API key needed) pytest tests/ -v # all tests including integration

Confirm every required deliverable exists:
☐ GitHub repository (public or accessible to organizer) ☐ At least one submission path: Live URL OR Docker image OR Code + RUNBOOK.md ☐ README.md with all required sections including MODELS ☐ requirements.txt (or pyproject.toml) ☐ tests/sample_output_case_01.json ☐ .env.example (no real secrets) ☐ GET /health returns { "status": "ok" } within 60 s of start ☐ POST /analyze-ticket responds within 30 s ☐ No stack traces or API keys in any HTTP response ☐ All enum values in responses match spec exactly ☐ Safety layer active — customer_reply never requests PIN/OTP/password ☐ RUNBOOK.md present for judge re-deployment
Optional but recommended: ☐ Architecture walkthrough video (≤ 90 seconds) submitted via form

prompt.verification: Final end-to-end run: pytest tests/ -m "not integration" -v # must be 100 % green pytest tests/test_sample_cases.py -v # run with real API key — aim for 8+/10 match Docker image builds and health check passes. README and RUNBOOK.md are complete and have no placeholder text.
prompt.do_not: - Do not commit a real ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the repository. - Do not leave placeholder text like "TODO" or "fill in later" in README or RUNBOOK. - Do not submit without testing the Docker path at least once. - Do not change enum values to match sample cases — they must match the spec.
prompt.final_note: Priority order if time runs short: 1. GET /health working (required for harness to start) 2. POST /analyze-ticket schema (15 % of score) 3. Evidence reasoning correct (35 % of score — highest weight) 4. Safety rules passing (20 % of score — disqualification risk) 5. Deployment reachable (5 % of score but required for any evaluation) 6. Documentation complete (5 % of score) A simple, reliable, safe API scores higher than a complex but broken one.
